mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand recovery across goods and durable assets, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a tightening cycle reveals supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during tightening periods or produces positive returns in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 2.5% annual threshold forces central banks to restrict liquidity, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation observations or the inflation threshold shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms broad-based labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, which systematically produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or GDP growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an early expansion phase signals improving capacity utilization and rising corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in >55% of early-cycle observations or loses predictive accuracy outside the specified regime.
